slides.title: The Magic Word: 'go' ✨
slides.content:
## Launching a Goroutine

In Go, an independently executing function is called a **Goroutine**.

To start a goroutine, you simply put the word `go` in front of a function call. It's that easy!

slides.title: Running in the Background 🏃
slides.content: Notice how the main program doesn't wait for `slowTask()` to finish. It immediately moves to the next line!

quizData.question: How do you tell Go to run a function named `fetchData()` concurrently in the background?
quizData.explanation: The simple word 'go' expertly launches a new goroutine uniquely to intelligently thoughtfully execute that safely gracefully brilliantly effectively beautifully function cleanly.

slides.title: The Main Goroutine ⚠️
slides.content:
## The Boss Program

Your `main()` function is the "main goroutine". If `main()` finishes and exits, it immediately kills all other goroutines running in the background, even if they aren't done yet!
